content.full_text:
Fain Polrrtcel PRACTrcss LoMMrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-s660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
April 4,2006
Andy Rogerson
Department of Transportation
5900 Folsom Boulevard
Sacramento, CA 9581 9-0128
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-06-036
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the statement of
economic interests provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
QUESTTONS
1. Are you required to report the costs for transportation,.and related lodging and
subsistence, paid for by the Federal Highway Administration to be a member of
an expert review panel that will conduct an assessment of labcratory research and
management practices at the Federal Highway Administration's Tumer-Fairbank
Highway Research Center in Mclean, Virginia, I:ur;re 19-22?
2. Does the Act prohibit you from accepting a $1,000 honorarium related to your
work on this panel?
3. If you are permitted to accept the honorarium, where on the Form 700 would you
be required to report it?
CONCLUSIONS
1. You are not required to report the costs associated with transportation, lodging,
or subsistence paid by the Federal Highway Administration on your Form 700,
Statement of Economic Interests.
2-3. You may accept the $1,000 honorarium offered to yori for your work on the
review panel and paid for by the Federal Highway Administration. You are not
required to report receipt of the honorarium on your Form 700.
' Gore*-"r,.t Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at T'itle 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulatioris.
,agg


File No. 4-06-036
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are the branch chief in charge of the Chemical Testing Branch within the
Division of Engineering Services of the Califomia Department of Transportation. You
have been asked to be a member of a review panel that will conduct an assessment of
laboratory research and malagement practices of the Coatings and Corrosion Laboratory
at the Federal Highway Administration's Tumer-Fairbank Highway Research Center in
Mclean, Virginia. In addition, you have been offered an honorarium of $1,000 for your
participation on the panel. All expenses, such as transportation, and related lodging and
subsistence, and the honorarium will be paid by the Federal Highway Administration, a
federal government agency. You intend to take vacation from your state position to
participate in the review panel.
Your state position is listed in the Department of Transportation's conflict-of-
interest code. Under your agency's code, you are required to report investments and
income from business entities that "entered into a contract with the Department or
engaged in the same business activity as the entity that entered into a contract with the
department to provide services, supplies, materials, machinery or equipment to the
department" during the reporting period and "such activities involve a section or unit in
which the designated employee performs functions." (Department of Transportation
regulation section 7000.)
ANALYSIS
Section 87300 requires that Department of Transportation adopt a Conflict of
Interest Code. Section 87302 requires that the code speciS, the positions in the agency
that involve the making or participation in the making of decisions that may foreseeably
have a material effect on any financial interest. Individuals holding these positions are
required to file periodic statements of economic interests. Your position is listed in your
agency's code. Therefore, you are required to complete the Form 700, Statement of
Economic Interests.
However, under the Department of Transportation's code, you have limited
disclosure with respect to the reporting of investments and income; you are only required
to report investments in and income from certain business entities. Section 82005 defines
a business entity as "any organization or enterprise operated for profit...." since
payrnents for your transportation, lodging, subsistence, and the honorarium will be made
by a federal government agency, these payments are not reportable by you.
A"gift" is defined in section 82028 as
"...any payment that confers a personal benefit on the
recipient, to the extent that consideration ofequal or greater
value is not received and includes a rebate or discount in
the price of anything of value unless the rebate or discount


File No. ,{-06-036
Page No. 3
is made in the regular course ofbusiness to members of the
public without regard to official status."
Gifts are a form of income and limited to $3602 per calendar year from a single
source if you would be required to report income and gifts from that source on your
statement of economic interests. (Section 89503(c).) In addition, section 89502(c)
prohibits the receipt of an honorarium if you would be required report the receipt of
income or gifts from the source of the honorarium. An honorarium is defined as a
payment made "in consideration for any speech given, article published, or attendance at
any public or private conference, convention, meeting, social event, meal, or like
gathering." (Section 89501(a).) Again, because a federal government agency is not a
source of income or gifts you are required to report under your disclosure category, the
payments you will receive are not subject to the gift limit or the honorarium prohibition.
Please note that we are unable to address whether these payments are reportable
to the Internal Revenue Service or the state's Franchise Tax Board as taxable income, or
whether the payment is subject to your agency's incompatible activities statement.
Please contact these agencies and your employer for further information.
If you have any other questions, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
By:
S. Moen, PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division
KSM:rd
I:\Adviceltrs\06-036
2 The gilt limit is adjusted every two years to reflect changes in the Consumer Price Index. The
$360 limit will be in effect through the end of 2006. (Section 89503(f); regulation 18940.2.)
